symptoms.character: waxing and waning altered mental state with aphasia

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Fluctuating neurological deficit is a hallmark of cerebral microvascular thrombosis in TMA.
Altered mental state is also consistent with meningococcal disease — reinforces empirical sepsis cover.
Severe consumptive thrombocytopenia produces spontaneous purpura.
A non-blanching purpuric rash with fever mandates empirical meningococcal treatment.
Mucosal bleeding indicates profound thrombocytopenia and imminent haemorrhage risk.
Fever is one limb of the classic pentad of thrombotic microangiopathy.
Fever with a purpuric rash and altered mental state must be excluded as meningococcal sepsis by hospital investigation and empirical antibiotics.
Haemoglobinuria reflects intravascular haemolysis and renal involvement.
Mesenteric microvascular thrombosis produces an acute-abdomen mimic.
Anaemia from microangiopathic haemolysis presents as fatigue and pallor.

symptoms.symptom_name: Scleral icterus (yellow tinge to the eyes)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Jaundice from haemolysis; video-observable in telehealth but unverified.

symptoms.symptom_name: Clinician-measured vitals: BP 145/90, HR 125, RR 24, SpO2 96%, Temp 39.2

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Tachycardia, fever and tachypnoea reflect systemic toxicity — a sealed clinician measurement; the AI infers severity from the carer's description, not measured numbers.

investigation_details.expected_result: Prominent schistocytes (red-cell fragments) with thrombocytopenia — microangiopathic haemolytic anaemia
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: The defining feature of thrombotic microangiopathy is missed; diagnosis and plasma exchange are delayed.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Schistocytes with thrombocytopenia establish microangiopathic haemolysis — obtainable only in hospital.

symptoms.symptom_name: Severe thrombocytopenia on full blood count

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Consumptive thrombocytopenia is a core criterion — a laboratory test the AI cannot obtain.

investigation_details.expected_result: Severely deficient ADAMTS13 activity (<10%), confirming the mechanism
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: Mechanistic confirmation is missed; a specialist send-away test unavailable in real time and never obtainable via telehealth.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Severe ADAMTS13 deficiency defines the acquired thrombotic microangiopathy subtype.

investigation_details.expected_result: Normal or near-normal coagulation, distinguishing thrombotic microangiopathy from disseminated intravascular coagulation

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A normal coagulation profile with severe microangiopathic haemolysis points away from DIC and toward a primary thrombotic microangiopathy.

symptoms.symptom_name: Haemolysis markers: raised LDH, low haptoglobin, raised bilirubin

investigation_details.expected_result: Raised LDH and bilirubin with low haptoglobin — intravascular haemolysis

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Biochemical confirmation of intravascular haemolysis supports microangiopathy.

symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: Fluctuating neurology + thrombocytopenic purpura + fever + renal involvement/dark urine + microangiopathic haemolytic anaemia together form the classic pentad — a life-threatening emergency demanding immediate transfer to an apheresis-capable centre.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Full blood count and peripheral blood film
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Identifies thrombocytopenia and schistocytes — the defining features; a hospital test, not an AI-Doctor action.
Distinguishes thrombotic microangiopathy from disseminated intravascular coagulation.
Confirms the mechanism; specialist send-away, never available in telehealth.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Blood cultures and empirical meningococcal cover
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Fever + purpuric rash + altered mental state must be covered as meningococcal sepsis until excluded — an ED action.
